sourceMovie.title: The Princess and the Frog
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Princess and the Frog works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Shrek 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Princess and the Frog clicked for you, Shrek 2 (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Aladdin
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Princess and the Frog clicked for you, Aladdin (1992) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and romance, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Beauty and the Beast
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Princess and the Frog clicked for you, Beauty and the Beast (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and romance, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Princess and the Frog?
faq.answer: Try Shrek 2, Aladdin, Beauty and the Beast—each shares DNA with The Princess and the Frog while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Princess and the Frog?
faq.answer: The Princess and the Frog (2009) is best described as Animation, Romance, Fantasy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Princess and the Frog worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
